title: pilosella lathraea
intro: Pilosella lathraea, also known as woolly hairgrass or wolf's bane, is a rare and highly toxic plant species belonging to the dandelion family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Pilosella lathraea are said to include treating digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing, although its toxicity and potential risks should be carefully considered. Herbal preparations of Pilosella lathraea are typically made from the roots and leaves, and are often used in homeopathic remedies, but should only be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
